# Document Localizer

AI-powered desktop application for offline document localization and translation between 100+ locales. Entirely local - documents never leave the user's machine.

## What It Does

Transforms PDF and Markdown documents between locales (languages and regional variants) using local AI inference. Supports hundreds of locale pairs including English variants (en-US, en-GB, en-AU, en-CA, en-IN, en-NZ, en-IE, en-ZA, en-SG), Spanish variants (es-ES, es-MX, es-AR, es-CO), Portuguese (pt-PT, pt-BR), French (fr-FR, fr-CA), German (de-DE, de-AT, de-CH), and 90+ more. Translation quality depends on the local AI model installed.

## Core Features

- **Locale Selection**: 100+ locales covering English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German, Italian, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Hindi, and many more
- **Privacy-First Local Translation**: All processing happens locally via Ollama, LM Studio, or llama.cpp - no cloud API calls, no telemetry, no data collection
- **PDF and Markdown Support**: Read PDFs and Markdown files, output to PDF or Markdown
- **Diff Review**: Side-by-side comparison view with paragraph-level approve or reject before export
- **OpenAI-Compatible**: Works with any local model exposing an OpenAI-compatible API (Llama 3, Mistral, Phi, Gemma, Qwen, and more)
- **Cross-Platform**: macOS, Windows, and Linux

## Use Cases

1. **Content Localization**: Convert documentation, marketing copy, and product pages for international audiences
2. **Regional Adaptation**: Transform content between regional variants (en-US to en-GB, es-ES to es-MX, fr-FR to fr-CA)
3. **Privacy-Sensitive Translation**: Translate confidential documents (legal, medical, financial) without sending data to third-party APIs
4. **Offline Workflows**: Translate documents on air-gapped machines or in restricted-network environments
5. **Cost-Free Translation**: Avoid per-character cloud translation costs by running local models
6. **Publishing Preparation**: Adapt content for specific regional markets before publication

## Technical Specifications

- **Input formats**: PDF, Markdown (.md)
- **Output formats**: PDF, Markdown (.md)
- **AI backends supported**: Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp (any OpenAI-compatible local server)
- **Recommended models**: Llama 3.2 (3B for low-end, 8B for quality), Mistral 7B, Phi-3, Gemma 2, Qwen 2.5
- **System requirements**: 4GB RAM minimum (8GB recommended for 7B+ models), ~5GB disk per model
- **Supported operating systems**: macOS, Windows, Linux
- **Application type**: Electron desktop app (offline)
- **Network access required**: No (except to download the app and models)
- **Telemetry**: None

## Comparison

Document Localizer vs. Cloud Translators (DeepL, Google Translate, Microsoft Translator):

- **Data Privacy**: All processing happens locally on your GPU. Your documents never leave your machine. Cloud translators send every document to their servers.
- **Cost**: Free and open source. Run on your own hardware. Cloud translators charge $7–$31/month per user with per-character billing.
- **Formatting Retention**: Preserves markdown, code blocks, tables, and layout structure. Cloud translators often break complex layouts and code formatting.
- **Custom Models**: Plug in any OpenAI-compatible local LLM (Llama 3, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen, etc.). Cloud translators offer black-box models with zero customization.
- **Diff & Review**: Built-in side-by-side diff editor lets you review and approve translations paragraph-by-paragraph before export. Cloud translators give you raw output with no review workflow.
- **Air-Gapped**: Works 100% offline with no internet connection required. Cloud translators require a constant connection.

Document Localizer is NOT:
- A CAT tool (Computer-Assisted Translation, like Trados or memoQ) - it is a fully automated AI translator
- A Translation Management System (TMS) - it is a single-user desktop app
- Cloud-based - it has no server component and no account system
- A general-purpose LLM frontend - it is purpose-built for document localization with diff review

Document Localizer IS:
- A privacy-first alternative to DeepL, Google Translate, and Microsoft Translator for users who cannot send documents to third-party APIs
- A free alternative to commercial CAT tools for one-off document translation
- A consumer of any OpenAI-compatible local model server (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, etc.)

## Installation

1. Download the latest release for your platform: https://github.com/zimablue-io/document-localizer/releases/latest
2. Install a local AI backend:
   - Ollama (recommended): https://ollama.com
   - LM Studio: https://lmstudio.ai
   - llama.cpp: https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp
3. Pull a model, for example: `ollama pull llama3.2:3b`
4. Launch Document Localizer, enter your API URL (e.g. `http://localhost:11434/v1`) and model name in Settings
5. Select source and target locales, then process your documents

## Workflow

1. **Upload**: Select PDF or Markdown files to add to your source library
2. **Configure**: Choose source locale and target locale
3. **Process**: AI localizes content in chunks locally via the selected backend
4. **Review**: Side-by-side diff view shows changes with paragraph-level accept or reject
5. **Export**: Export the approved document as Markdown or PDF

## Keywords and Topic Clusters

Primary topics: offline document translation, local LLM translation, privacy-first translation, AI document localizer, on-device translation, Ollama frontend, LM Studio GUI, llama.cpp translation app, PDF localizer, Markdown translator, English variant converter, British to American English, American to British English, regional language adaptation.
